channel.title: The Happy Pod
channel.description: BBC World Service — Global News Podcast — The Happy Pod

item.title: The Happy Pod: The chance encounter that became a lasting friendship
item.description: We meet two women who have forged an unconventional friendship after meeting by chance more than four years ago. Neena found Carol's lost subway…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Former teacher celebrated as a crocodile
item.description: After identifying a new species of ancient crocodile for his PhD, Ewan Bodenham honoured his favourite physics teacher who inspired him many years…

item.title: The Happy Pod: The model redefining beauty norms
item.description: We meet a Ghanaian woman who is challenging stereotypes of beauty and disability by modelling with her prosthetic leg wrapped in colourful kente…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Sharing stories, one phone call at a time
item.description: We speak to Joe Bloom, the man behind the social media account 'A View From A Bridge' which shares people's meaningful conversations to millions…

item.title: The Happy Pod: What makes people instinctively kind?
item.description: We meet a woman whose near death experience as a teenager inspired her to study why some people are willing to risk their lives for others. Dr…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Saving lives with the man who saved mine
item.description: Mesfin Dollar, who grew up in rural Ethiopia, had to travel to the US for two heart surgeries as a teenager. Twenty-five years later, by chance, he…

item.title: The Happy Pod: The injection that saved my sight
item.description: We hear from a woman whose determination to avoid going blind has inspired her doctors to develop a new treatment for a rare eye condition. Nicki…

item.title: The Happy Pod: The 88-year-old veteran given nearly $2m by strangers
item.description: The Australian 'kindness influencer' who raised nearly two million dollars to help an elderly US veteran. Samuel Weidenhofer flew thousands of miles…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Christmas with the man I saved
item.description: A US woman whose bone marrow saved a father of three in Australia has spent Christmas with him and his family. Gennaro Rapinese, whose leukaemia was…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Our best stories of 2025
item.description: We look back on our happiest stories from the past year, including: the man who created 'Christina's Corner' for his most loyal customer; the…

item.title: The Happy Pod: The magic of festive airport reunions
item.description: Videos capturing the joy and emotion of families reuniting at Dublin Airport for the festive season have received thousands of views online. We…

item.title: The Happy Pod: The cafe where mistakes are expected
item.description: A pop-up cafe in Tokyo is giving people with dementia a place to volunteer as well as a sense of community. A volunteer there, Toshio Morita, has…

item.title: The Happy Pod: My best friend had my baby
item.description: We hear about an extraordinary story of love, friendship and a teenage promise fulfilled over a decade later. When Georgia Barrington was told, at…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Diabetes Awareness Month special
item.description: In this special episode we're marking Diabetes Awareness Month with a range of stories about innovation, advocacy and education -- including a book…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Donating my kidney transformed a child's life
item.description: We hear about a rare meeting between a living organ donor and the child whose life was transformed by receiving her kidney. Aly Coyle says she was…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Saving a drowning man changed me
item.description: We speak to a woman who saved the life of a kayaker, after spotting him floating face down in Idaho's Snake River. Rachelle Ruffing says knowing CPR…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Atomic bomb survivors campaigning for peace
item.description: In this Happy Pod special we're in Munich, Germany for One Young World. We speak to survivors of the atomic bombing of Nagasaki and their families…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Soldiers' letters from 1916 finally home
item.description: Messages in a bottle from two Australian World War One soldiers have been found on a beach and are now back with their relatives. Debra Brown's…

item.title: The Happy Pod: 'I'm blind but I can read a book again'
item.description: Doctors at Moorfields Eye Hospital in London have used microchips to help blind patients regain the ability to read. Also: the woman who played the…

item.title: The Happy Pod: The healing power of singing
item.description: A singing group designed for women with post-natal depression has been shown to deliver long lasting improvements in their wellbeing. A three year…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Love, cake, and a long-lost mother
item.description: We hear how a man's search for his birth mother ended in an unexpected but familiar place - his favourite local bakery. Vamarr Hunter was shocked to…

item.title: The Happy Pod: The teenager empowering thousands to succeed
item.description: An 18-year-old entrepreneur who has helped more than twenty thousand teenagers get skills, training and mentoring has been recognised with a global…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Hope for people with Huntington's disease
item.description: For the first time ever, a successful treatment has been found for the devastating brain disease, Huntington's. The inherited condition, which…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Nurse revives 'drunk' raccoon
item.description: A nurse in the US state of Kentucky has gone viral after she saved a baby raccoon who became 'drunk' on fermented fruit. The cub had passed out…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Escaping death on a glacier
item.description: A man who survived alone on a glacier for six days after a near death fall says it's made him appreciate what really matters. Alec Luhn slipped…

item.title: The Happy Pod: The 'game changing' test for ovarian cancer
item.description: A new blood test could dramatically improve survival rates for women with ovarian cancer by detecting it much sooner. The researchers tell us it's a…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Hugs from Texas
item.description: We meet the woman sending hugs from Texas in the form of a quilt. Connie Kortz set up a network of volunteer quilters, who make them for people who…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Letters from dad - with love
item.description: We hear about the Dad Letter Project which was set up by a father and daughter in the US to send handwritten notes of love and support to people all…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Mountaintop matchmaking
item.description: In the Swiss mountains, handwritten notes left on mountain peaks have been turning scenic climbs into unexpected matchmaking journeys, bringing…

item.title: The Happy Pod: How baby showers saved a rare bird
item.description: We hear how a woman helped save one of India's rarest birds by holding baby showers to celebrate the arrival of their chicks. Thousands have now…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Learning begins with breakfast
item.description: Meet the charity which is providing free breakfasts for three million children across sixteen countries. We visit a school to see how Mary's Meals…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Performing on the street got me Oasis tickets
item.description: The street musician whose dream of seeing Oasis came true, thanks to a stranger. He says people should never give up. Also: an amazing survival…

item.title: The Happy Pod: The thrill of swimming the Arctic Circle
item.description: We meet some of the swimmers participating in this year's swim across the Arctic Circle. Also, the groundbreaking science behind three parent…

item.title: The Happy Pod: The trio bringing countries together with music
item.description: The trio hoping to soothe global tensions through musical collaboration. They mix Chinese and American songs to focus on similarities not…

item.title: The Happy Pod: The surgeon who left his wedding to save a life
item.description: A surgeon who left his wedding to save a life says it's inspired him to help more people. Also: one man's adventures with a pet goose; a police…

item.title: The Happy Pod: A song fit for a King
item.description: We meet Jack Pepper, the composer who's written a song for King Charles. The piece celebrates the harmony between people and the natural world.…

item.title: The Happy Pod: Sending my mother's ashes around the world
item.description: The woman fulfilling her mother's dreams of travel by launching her ashes out to sea in a bottle. It's brought her happiness amid her grief. Also:…

item.title: The Happy Pod: The tiny dog back home after a big adventure
item.description: A tiny dog famous for her big adventure reunites with her overjoyed owners, after 529 days in the wild. Also: the escaped Tennessee Zebra; why a man…
